name: Shrayansh Tiwari
title: Biological Sciences & Computation
tagline: Studying biological systems at the interface of computational modeling and neurobiology.
narrative: I am an undergraduate student in biological sciences at IISER Pune. I build computational models and write code to explore biological complexity. To me, a computer is a microscope—a tool to model, simulate, and understand biological systems that are too small, fast, or intricate to analyze in isolation.

When I am not in the lab or at a computer terminal, I run. Long-distance running is where physiology and computation meet in real-time: a biological optimization problem of energy pacing, cardiorespiratory load, and biomechanics. I also organize the Ascent Run Club, a community of endurance runners in Pune where we share trails and steady miles.

This website is a quiet, personal space on the internet. I prefer libraries over cafés, well-made notebooks over social feeds, and spend my time learning new things and writing rather than shouting about achievements. I build projects to satisfy my curiosity and structure my thoughts.
